questions.question: Who coined the term 'Responsive Web Design'?
questions.explanation: Ethan Marcotte coined the term in his 2010 article on A List Apart.

questions.question: Which meta tag is essential for responsive design on mobile devices?
questions.explanation: The viewport meta tag controls layout on mobile browsers.

questions.question: What does 'width=device-width' in the viewport meta tag do?
options: Sets the page to desktop width
Sets page width to device screen width
Sets the device width to page width
questions.explanation: It matches the page width to the device's screen width.

questions.question: Which CSS unit is NOT considered a relative unit?
questions.explanation: px (pixels) is an absolute unit, while %, em, and vw are relative.

questions.question: What does 'max-width: 100%' on an image achieve?
options: Prevents image from exceeding its container width
Makes image responsive in height only
questions.explanation: It ensures the image scales down to fit its container but never exceeds its natural size.

questions.question: Which CSS feature automatically creates as many columns as will fit?
questions.explanation: auto-fit with minmax creates flexible columns that wrap automatically.

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum touch target size for accessibility?
questions.explanation: WCAG recommends at least 44x44px for touch targets.

questions.question: Which approach is recommended for responsive CSS?
questions.explanation: Mobile-first builds from smallest screen up, ensuring progressive enhancement.

questions.question: Which CSS property creates fluid typography that scales between minimum and maximum values?
questions.explanation: clamp(MIN, VAL, MAX) creates fluid typography within bounds.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'fr' unit in CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: fr represents a fraction of the available space in the grid container.

questions.question: Which pattern is best for showing/hiding navigation on mobile?
questions.explanation: Off Canvas slides navigation off-screen, common in mobile menus.

questions.question: What does 'initial-scale=1.0' in the viewport tag do?
questions.explanation: It sets the initial zoom level to 1:1 (no zoom).

questions.question: Which CSS property helps prevent layout shift from images?
questions.explanation: aspect-ratio reserves space for images before they load, reducing layout shift.

questions.question: What is the default viewport width for mobile browsers without the viewport meta tag?
questions.explanation: Without the viewport tag, mobile browsers default to 980px and zoom out.

questions.question: Which media query approach is best for mobile-first design?
questions.explanation: min-width queries layer on styles as viewport grows, supporting mobile-first.

questions.question: What is the benefit of 'content-visibility: auto'?
questions.explanation: It defers rendering of off-screen sections, improving initial load performance.

questions.question: Which responsive pattern involves content reordering at breakpoints?
questions.explanation: Layout Shifter moves content blocks to different positions at different breakpoints.

questions.question: What is the role of 'object-fit: cover' on images?
options: Crops image to fill container while maintaining aspect ratio
questions.explanation: object-fit: cover crops the image to fill the container while preserving aspect ratio.

questions.question: Which is NOT a benefit of responsive images with srcset?
questions.explanation: srcset can increase HTML size, but the bandwidth savings from serving correct sizes outweigh this.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'prefers-reduced-data' media query?
options: Detects data saver mode on devices
questions.explanation: prefers-reduced-data detects when the user has enabled data-saving mode.
